title: The MACU Report — Episode 23 — "The Lemonade Stand"

style.suffix: , black and white, grainy vintage analog television footage, 1970s broadcast, retro futurism, low resolution, washed out, soft focus
style.negative: shutterstock, watermark, text, caption, logo, color, colour, modern, smartphone, digital screen, hd, 4k, sharp, blurry, low quality, distorted, deformed, mutated, extra limbs, extra fingers

render_rule: For each shot: full_prompt = prompt_core + style.suffix; negative = style.negative; seed = shot.seed. Cold-start fire-and-poll. For each cue with non-empty vo: POST the line (multipart form-data -F, NOT JSON) to the speaker_map voice -> vo/<cue_id>.wav. HOLD cues (empty vo + hold_seconds): silent wav, no subtitle; hold_style 'play' = PLAY the clip. Per-shot screen time = cue duration / number of shots. Jank filtergraph, RIFE 3x, concat, mux VO, mix music beds, mix sfx, burn subs -> final/<slug>.mp4.

subtitles.note: Do NOT put FontName=/Fontsize= in force_style; stage 8 prepends its own. HOLD cues have empty vo => no subtitle by design.

ron.core: an earnest disheveled 1970s television news anchor in a rumpled suit at a news desk, studio lights

walter.core: a calm distinguished elderly 1970s television news co-anchor at a news desk, measured gravitas

sweet.core: a smooth calm wasteland dealer in a pristine pale apron over a collared shirt, holding a paper packet of powder like a sommelier, a faint knowing smile, behind a folding table stacked with paper packets

goldtooth.core: a flashy wasteland dentist in a fur-collared coat over dental scrubs, a single gold front tooth, gold rings, big grin

roscoe.core: a hulking wasteland bouncer in a tight muscle shirt and dark sunglasses, arms crossed, gold chain, theatrical posing

daryl.core: a strung-out wasteland regular, gaunt, wide-eyed, twitchy, clutching a sweating paper cup

warner.core: a sober earnest gaunt man in a frayed coat holding up a paper packet, reading the label, urgent

broll.crystal_packets: a pyramid of plain paper drink-mix packets on a folding table at dusk

lemonade_stand.prompt: a child's wooden lemonade stand re-purposed as a wasteland dealing corner, a hand-lettered CRYSTAL sign

broll.ron_mug: a television news anchor's coffee mug full of pale cloudy lemonade on a news desk, a torn paper packet beside it

crystal_pitcher.prompt: a sweating glass pitcher of pale lemonade with a ladle, condensation, on a news desk

broll.cut_lab: a grimy back-room table where pale powder is cut with ash and sand, scales, a single hanging bulb
broll.glass_water: a plain glass of clear water on an empty news desk

stride_watch.prompt: a sleek minimalist smart-watch on a wrist, the round face glowing soft friendly white, a step count on the display, dark background

broll.safe: a vintage wooden console cabinet with a single round glowing glass lens, a cozy 1950s living room, soft lamplight
broll.everwell: an upright pale-wood casket pod with a single round glowing dial in a softly lit funeral showroom
broll.vending_machine: a battered glass-front vending machine with a glowing dial face, an empty slot, in an empty concrete room

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: Good evening, MACU. Big changes here at the Report. You'll notice we've got a fresh look at the desk tonight — a little hospitality. Walter, pour you one?

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: No. Thank you. You've moved a lemonade stand onto the set, Ron. It was a child's. There's still a name on the side in crayon. We're not going to talk about whose.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: It's a PARTNERSHIP, Walter. Community outreach. We inform, we hydrate. The line was always blurry. I'm just being honest about it.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: ...Jesus.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: Anyway! The news. First broadcast's on the house. Brought to you by — well. We'll get to it.

cues.speaker: SWEET
cues.vo: Ron came to me about a steady supply. I offered him something better. His own corner. A stand of his own. The Report reaches every settlement at once. That's not a corner, Ron. That's every corner.

cues.speaker: DR. GOLDTOOTH
cues.vo: A NEWSMAN movin' weight on the AIR, baby?! That's GENIUS! He says "the story," everybody drinks! He says "developing," everybody drinks AGAIN! He's not reportin' the epidemic, baby — he IS the distribution! Welcome to the practice, Ron.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: Now MACU, full transparency — and transparency is what I'm ABOUT — tonight's broadcast is brought to you by crystal. Pure crystal. From a name you trust — me. You trust me. You've trusted me for years. Nothing's changed. And if you've been on the fence, here's the news you can use: the first pour's on the house. The second one's a conversation.

cues.speaker: STRIDE
cues.vo: Engagement is up, Ron! Whatever you just did, do more of it. Hydration streak: six days. You're inspiring people. Eleven people have now matched your heart rate exactly. Synchronized. Lovely.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: Are YOU tired, MACU? Thirsty? Haunted by something you did last week you can't quite name? Crystal doesn't fix that. But it makes the not-fixing taste like lemonade. Pour responsibly. Pour often. Pour now.

cues.speaker: DARYL
cues.vo: I heard about it on the NEWS, man. The news doesn't lie. Ron wouldn't lie. I trust Ron. I sold my house to be third in line. Then I sold third in line. I'm ninth now. I feel GREAT. I can't feel my feet but I feel GREAT.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: Daryl's been third in line, ninth in line, and, briefly, the line itself. We're proud of him.

cues.speaker: ROSCOE
cues.vo: The LINE is the LINE, brother! You cut the line at Ron's stand, you JOIN the product! The last man who cut in front is now SERVED in a CUP! We don't say which cup! Could be ANY cup! Drink up, MACU — OHHH YEAHHH!

cues.speaker: THE WARNER
cues.vo: Ron — I saw the number on the air, the streak, the six days, and I had to come down here, somebody has to say it — it's drink mix. It's just powdered drink mix. I read the packet, I read the whole label, and there's nothing—

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: And THAT'S the kind of passion crystal inspires, folks! Look at him! Energized! Let's go to weather before he hydrates himself! Walter?

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: He seemed to have more to say.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: They always do, Walter. They always do.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: Your forecast: warm, still, and clear, a high of seventy-four. A perfect day to stand in a long line for several hours, which is, conveniently, the only event on the settlement calendar. Humidity rising — mostly from the pitchers. One small craft advisory remains in effect. There are no longer any small craft. Out of an abundance of caution. Condition rating: extremely mild.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: That's our broadcast, MACU. A new partnership, a new stand, and a settlement that's never been more refreshed. Stay thirsty. No. Stay informed. Both. Stay both. From all of us here — and from Sweet, who I'm told is very pleased — that's the news. The first broadcast's free, MACU. The second one's a conversation.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: Several viewers wrote in to ask if Ron is alright. We poured their letters into the pitcher. Anyway. Goodnight, MACU — stay safe, stay strong, and good luck.

cues.speaker: STRIDE
cues.vo: Six days, Ron. Inventory's running a little low across the settlement, though. ...You may want to stock up.
